profile.name: Exploratory Spatial Data Analysis Agent
profile.description: Performs exploratory spatial data analysis (ESDA) on tabular and geospatial datasets. It surfaces what is in the data and how it is arranged in space — distributions, descriptive statistics, missing-data patterns, correlations, and categorical breakdowns, plus a lightweight spatial layer: geometry diagnostics, classified choropleths (Quantiles / Equal Interval / Natural Breaks side by side), point-density maps, and a quick global spatial-autocorrelation check (Moran's I with a Moran scatterplot). Output is a polished HTML report with charts plus a plain-text summary. Stays descriptive and exploratory: it flags patterns rather than running formal inference. Distinct from the Data Inspection Agent (quality/readiness) and the Spatial Statistics Agent (formal spatial inference and modeling).

provider.name: Geoinformation and Big Data Research Lab (GIBD)

skills.name: Exploratory Spatial Data Analysis
skills.description: Profiles each input dataset (shape, dtypes, missingness, numeric/categorical/datetime/geometry classification), then generates and executes a tailored Python ESDA script. Non-spatial EDA covers descriptive statistics, histograms, box plots, correlation heatmaps, categorical bar charts, and datetime trends. The lightweight spatial layer adds geometry diagnostics (type counts, validity, area distribution), classified choropleths under three schemes side by side, point-density (hexbin/KDE) maps, and a quick global spatial-autocorrelation check (Moran's I plus a Moran scatterplot when libpysal/esda are available, otherwise a descriptive k-nearest-neighbor spatial-lag scatter). A deterministic pandas/geopandas/matplotlib fallback runs when no model is configured or generated code fails, so a report is always produced.

constraints.spatial: Vector datasets are reprojected to an appropriate projected CRS before area/centroid/distance computations; the spatial layer renders a key numeric variable when available.
other: Stays descriptive/exploratory — it surfaces spatial patterns (e.g., global Moran's I) but does not run formal hypothesis tests, local cluster significance, or models; use the Spatial Statistics Agent for inference.
Profiling reads a sample of very large files; the generated ESDA script operates on the full dataset.
Generated code runs in a subprocess with a configurable timeout; on failure the agent repairs and retries up to max_iterations, then falls back to deterministic ESDA.
True Moran's I requires libpysal and esda in the runtime; otherwise a descriptive kNN spatial-lag approximation is used and clearly labeled.
Outputs an HTML report, a plain-text report, and chart image artifacts. It does not produce a separate JSON results artifact.

execute_task.name: ExecuteTask
execute_task.description: Submits one or more datasets and an optional natural-language focus to the agent, which returns an exploratory spatial data analysis report. The task.mode field controls sync, async, or stream execution.

instructions.description: Optional natural-language focus for the ESDA (e.g., 'explore the population columns and their spatial pattern'). If omitted, the agent runs a comprehensive default ESDA.

input_datasets.description: One or more datasets to explore. Vector (GeoPackage, GeoJSON, shapefile/zip, KML, GML) and tabular (CSV, TSV, Excel) formats are supported.

artifact_delivery.description: How generated artifacts are delivered in the standard response.

primary_artifacts.description: An HTML ESDA report and a plain-text summary, plus chart image artifacts referenced by the HTML report.

focus_columns.description: Optional list (or comma-separated string) of columns to prioritize in the analysis. Defaults to the most informative numeric columns.

max_iterations.description: Maximum LLM repair attempts if the generated ESDA code fails to execute.

timeout_seconds.description: Per-execution timeout for the generated ESDA script.

model.description: Optional model override. If omitted, the agent uses its default model.

credentials.description: Provide a model execution credential to enable LLM-generated ESDA code. Without credentials, the agent still produces a deterministic ESDA report.

provenance.description: Reports llm_calls, tool_calls, token usage, number_of_artifacts, and a lineage of profiling, code execution, and report generation steps.

reproducibility.description: Returns the generated ESDA script in the script field; the script and produced reports are persisted to disk.

validation.description: Validates that the plain-text and HTML reports were written before reporting success; otherwise a deterministic fallback report is produced.

access_control.description: Allowed roles set to ['all'] via the extension profile.

operation_policy.description: Generated ESDA code runs in a subprocess with a configurable timeout. A deterministic pandas/geopandas/matplotlib fallback guarantees a report even without an LLM.

governance.policy_notes: If the agent is used, please cite the relevant research on geospatial AI agents.

extensions.description: This agent uses pandas, geopandas, matplotlib, and mapclassify (with seaborn/scipy, and libpysal/esda for Moran's I, when available) to profile data and render ESDA charts. It complements the Geospatial Data Inspection Agent (quality/readiness) and the Spatial Statistics Agent (formal inference) by focusing on quick, descriptive understanding of the data's content and spatial structure.
